Wave Merge Tool (WavMerge)

WavMerge combines mono wave files into a 4-channel or 6-channel WaveFormatEx interleaved PCM wave file. The bit depths and sampling rates of the mono wave files must be identical. Their durations, however, may differ. The output file's duration will equal that of the longest input file. You may pass "." to denote silence for a channel.

The first MIDI or DLS loop chunk found in a wave file will be persisted to the output file. Subsequent chunks will be ignored and a warning will be printed.

WavMerge also persists all additional RIFF chunks found in source files that are not 'fmt ' or 'data' chunks. The exception, as stated above, are loop chunks ('smpl' or 'wsmp'). Only the first loop chunk found will be persisted.

USAGE: wavmerge [leftchan | .] [rightchan | .] [centerchan | .] [lfechan | .] [leftrearchan | .] [rightrearchan | .] outfile
Option Description
leftchan The left channel.
rightchan The right channel.
centerchan The center channel.
lfechan The LFE channel.
leftrearchan The left, rear channel.
rightrearchan The right, rear channel.
. Used to denote silence.
outfile The output wave file.
-? Display usage.

Example Usage

To create a six-channel wave file with the left channel audible and silence in the other channels:

wavmerge left.wav . . . . . result.wav

To create a six-channel wave file with the right channel audible and silence in the other channels:

wavmerge . right.wav . . . . result.wav
